issue.message: Class referenced in the layout file, `com.google.android.exoplayer2.ui.PlayerView`, was not found in the project or the libraries
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Found dynamic locale changes, but did not find corresponding Play Core library calls for downloading languages and splitting by language is not disabled in the `bundle` configuration
The application should not provide its own launch screen

When using `setHasFixedSize() in an `RecyclerView`, `wrap_content` cannot be used as a value for `size` in the scrolling direction.

Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `FirebaseFirestore` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
issue.errorLine1: private static final FirebaseFirestore db = FirebaseFirestore.getInstance();

issue.message: Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `VolleySingleton` which has field `ctx` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields; this is a memory leak

When you define `paddingStart` you should probably also define `paddingEnd` for right-to-left symmetry
